name: TextGrad (Automatic Prompt Optimization via Backprop)

performance.notes: Multiple LLM API calls per optimization step; cost per iteration can be high

agent_readiness.notes: Automatic prompt optimization treating LLMs as differentiable functions (text backpropagation). import textgrad as tg. tg.set_backward_engine('gpt-4o'). system_prompt = tg.Variable('You are...', requires_grad=True). loss_fn = tg.TextLoss('Is the response helpful?'). optimizer = tg.TGD(parameters=[system_prompt]). loss = loss_fn(model_response); loss.backward(); optimizer.step(). Optimizer uses LLM critique to improve prompts. Stanford research paper (2024). Early stage — API may change. Expensive: multiple GPT-4 calls per step. Alternative to DSPy for prompt optimization.

gotchas: Very early stage — API changes frequently
Expensive: 3-10x LLM API calls vs non-optimized prompts
Non-deterministic optimization — results vary between runs
Smaller community than DSPy for prompt optimization
best_when: Automatic prompt optimization when DSPy is too complex
When gradient-based intuition helps team understand optimization
Simple prompt optimization tasks not requiring DSPy's full pipeline
avoid_when: Cost-sensitive applications (multiple LLM calls per step)
summary: TextGrad applies automatic differentiation to text — optimizes prompts via LLM backpropagation. tg.Variable(prompt, requires_grad=True). loss = loss_fn(response); loss.backward(); optimizer.step() — LLM critiques improve prompt. Multiple LLM calls per optimization step (expensive). Stanford Zou Lab (2024). Early stage and API unstable. For production prompt optimization use DSPy. Best for research experiments with gradient-based prompt optimization.
